zh.lifeSummary: 當Maria Elizabeth Wagner於1775年出生在美國北卡羅來納州奧拉時，她的父親Conrad Waggoner46歲，母親Eva Margaretha Kale38歲。她和Henry Cagle至少有8個兒子和4個女兒。她於1850年住在美國田納西州德卡度。她去世於1829年在美國田納西州哈丁享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wagner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Wägner): occupational name for a carter and (in some dialects) a cartwright, from an agent derivative of Middle High German wagen ‘cart, wagon’, German Wagen. This surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Britain, Poland, and Denmark. In Hungary it is mostly spelled Wágner and Vágner. In Russia, Czechia, Croatia, Slovenia, and Slovakia it is also found in the Slavicized form Vagner . Compare Wagener , Waggener , and Wagoner .

Dutch and perhaps also English: occupational name from Middle Dutch waghenaer ‘carter’ (compare 1 above). The Dutch word is not known to have been borrowed into English before 1600 but the surname Wagner is recorded in Norfolk (England) from 1379, perhaps a substitution of the Dutch word for Middle English wainer. Compare Waggoner .

variants.name: Waggener
meanings: Americanized form of German   Wagner   or its variant   Wagener  . Compare   Waggoner  .

variants.name: Wagler
meanings: German: variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wanger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Aloysius, Frieda, Heinz, Kurt, Manfred.
German:
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wang (Bavaria, Austria) or Wangen.

variants.name: Waggoner
meanings: Americanized form of German or Dutch   Wagner   or its German and Dutch variants   Wagener   and   Wagenaar  . Compare   Waggener   and   Wagoner  .
Possibly also English: altered form of Waghorn . The surname Waggoner is very rare in Britain.

History: A planter named John Waggener or Waggoner, who came to America c. 1670 and lived in Essex County, VA, is said to have been born in Colchester, Essex (England). No documentary source for this origin is given and an English origin is difficult to establish. The nearest similar English surname is Wagner 2, a very rare Norfolk name, originating in the 14th-century. In 17th-century Kent a number of families named Waghorn alias Wagon were occasionally also known as Waggoner (through false association with that word). Direct derivation from the English word wag(g)on is not possible, since it was borrowed into English only in the 16th century from Dutch, and wag(g)oner is an early Modern English coinage.

variants.name: Waner
meanings: Swabian habitational name for someone from a place called Wain, or from Wahn near Cologne.
possibly an altered form of Wähner, a variant of Wagner .
German (Wäner):

variants.name: Vagner
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic), Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian; Hungarian (Vágner); Slovak (also Vágner): occupational name for a carter or cartwright, of German origin (see Wagner ), often applied as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic occupational names or surnames.

variants.name: Wegner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Dieter, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Helmuth, Bernhard, Ernst, Hans, Alfons, Eldor.
German: northern and central variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wagoner
meanings: Americanized form of German or Dutch   Wagner   or its German and Dutch variants   Wagener   and   Wagenaar  . Compare   Waggoner  .
Possibly also English: variant of Waggoner . The surname Wagoner is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Wagener
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Fritz, Arno, Bernhard, Dieter, Horst, Johann, Klaus, Kurt, Matthias, Willi.
German: variant of   Wagner  .

variants.name: Wegener
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Heinz, Friedrich, Johannes, Lorenz, Armin, Eldor, Erna, Guenther, Hans, Kurt, Manfred.
German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wagner  .

nameMeanings.name: Maria
meanings: A name derived from Mapia, one of the Greek forms of Miriam. Maria is found as a male name as well as a female name. Viscount Montague, of Cowdray, Sussex, who succeeded to the title as second viscount, was christened Anthony Maria Browne. In Camden's Remains (p. 44), referred to in Delicie Literarise, occurs Thomas Maria Wingfield; and there was also a Sir Edward Maria Wingfield. The name appears to have been adopted in the latter family from Mary, daughter of Henry VIII. Compare Notes and Queries 5th S. ii. 73, 316, 478. Marie is frequently found as a male name in France and in other parts of the Continent.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: Whiskey Rebellion Occurs
historicEvents.description: The Whiskey Rebellion was a protest against taxes on whiskey by farmers and distillers. People were tarred and feathered. They attacked Bower Hill and headed towards Pittsburgh. The militia was sent in to try and stop it. It finally ended with a repeal of the tax.
Lewis and Clark set out from St. Louis, MO to explore the West.

historicEvents.title: Walton War
historicEvents.description: The Walton War was a border dispute between Georgia and North Carolina, when Georgia established Walton County. The main piece of land being fought over was called the Orphan Strip and was located between North Carolina and Georgia. It started in 1804 and became part of the War of 1812 and lasted until 1818.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The War Between Georgia and North Carolina

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

en.lifeSummary: When Maria Elizabeth Wagner was born in 1775, in Orange, North Carolina, United States, her father, Conrad Waggoner, was 46 and her mother, Eva Margaretha Kale, was 38. She had at least 8 sons and 4 daughters with Henry Cagle. She lived in Decatur, Tennessee, United States in 1850. She died in 1829, in Hardin, Tennessee, United States, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maria Elizabeth Wagner est née en 1775, à Orange, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, son père, Conrad Waggoner, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Eva Margaretha Kale, avait 38 ans. Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles avec Henry Cagle. Elle a vécu à Decatur, Tennessee, États-Unis en 1850. Elle est décédée en 1829, à Hardin, Tennessee, États-Unis, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maria Elizabeth Wagner 1775 in Orange, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Conrad Waggoner 46 und ihre Mutter Eva Margaretha Kale 38. Sie hatte mit Henry Cagle mindestens 8 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1850 hat sie in Decatur, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb 1829 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Hardin, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maria Elizabeth Wagner è nata il nel 1775 nel Orange, Carolina del Nord, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Conrad Waggoner, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Eva Margaretha Kale, aveva 38 anni. Lei avuto almeno 8 figli e 4 figlie con Henry Cagle. Vissuto a Decatur, Tennessee, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850. Morì nel 1829, nel Hardin, Tennessee, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maria Elizabeth Wagnerが1775年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州オランッジで生まれた時，父Conrad Waggonerは46歳，母Eva Margaretha Kaleは38歳であった。彼女はHenry Cagleとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国テネシー州デカトゥルに住んでいた。彼女は1829年にアメリカ合衆国テネシー州ハーディンで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maria Elizabeth Wagner(이)가 1775년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 오랑에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Conrad Waggoner(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eva Margaretha Kale(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 Henry Cagle(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 테네시 주 데카투르에서 살았다. 그녀는 1829년 미국 테네시 주 하르딘에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maria Elizabeth Wagner nasceu em 1775, em Orange, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Conrad Waggoner, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Eva Margaretha Kale, tinha 38 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 8 filhos e 4 filhas com Henry Cagle. Ela viveu em Decatur, Tenessi, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ela faleceu em 1829, em Hardin, Tenessi, Estados Unidos da América, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maria Elizabeth Wagner родилась в 1775 г. в местности оранг, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Conrad Waggoner было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Eva Margaretha Kale, 38. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери от Henry Cagle. Она жила в местности декатур, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла в 1829 г. в местности хардин, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maria Elizabeth Wagner nació en 1775, en Orange, Carolina del Norte, Estados Unidos, su papá, Conrad Waggoner, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Eva Margaretha Kale, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 8 hijos y 4 hijas con Henry Cagle. Vivió en Decatur, Tennessee, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió en 1829, en Hardin, Tennessee, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 54 años.
